mnemonics.title: Linked List Basics
mnemonics.content: NODE = DATA + NEXT POINTER - THINK OF A TRAIN WHERE EACH COACH (NODE) HAS PASSENGERS (DATA) AND A COUPLING (NEXT POINTER) TO THE NEXT COACH

mnemonics.title: Insertion Order
mnemonics.content: INSERT AT HEAD: NEW→HEAD, HEAD=NEW - PICTURE ADDING A NEW FIRST IN A CONVOY - THE NEW VEHICLE BECOMES THE LEADER

mnemonics.title: Delete Operation
mnemonics.content: TO DELETE: SAVE NODE, BYPASS IT WITH POINTERS, FREE MEMORY - LIKE SKIPPING A PAGE IN A CHAIN OF PAGES BY CONNECTING PAGE 3 DIRECTLY TO PAGE 5

acronyms.usage: Stack implementation using linked lists - last inserted node is first to be deleted
Queue implementation using linked lists - first inserted node is first to be deleted
The next pointer of the last node is set to NULL to indicate end of list
HEAD is a pointer variable that stores address of the first node in the list

visualTips.visualization: DRAW A BOX SPLIT INTO TWO: LEFT HALF = DATA, RIGHT HALF = POINTER ARROW POINTING TO NEXT BOX. USE ARROW HEAD TO SHOW DIRECTION.
DRAW THREE NODES: A→B→C. INSERT NEW NODE X AT BEGIN: DRAW X WITH ARROW TO A, THEN REDRAW HEAD ARROW FROM X TO A. SHOW HOW X NOW COMES FIRST.
DRAW A PATH WITH ARROWS FROM NODE 1 TO NODE 2 TO NODE 3. HIGHLIGHT EACH NODE AS YOU MOVE ALONG. IMAGINE A BALL ROLLING FOLLOWING THE ARROWS.
DRAW A CLOCK FACE. ALL NUMBERS ARE NODES. ARROWS GO CLOCKWISE. THE ARROW FROM 12 (LAST) BACK TO 1 (FIRST) SHOWS THE CIRCULAR CONNECTION.
DRAW NODES AS SQUARES WITH THREE SECTIONS: LEFT ARROW (PREV), CENTER (DATA), RIGHT ARROW (NEXT). ARROWS ALTERNATE LEFT-RIGHT SHOWING BIDIRECTIONAL LINKS.

analogies.analogy: LINKED LIST IS LIKE A TREASURE HUNT WHERE EACH CLUE (NODE) POINTS TO THE NEXT LOCATION. ARRAY IS LIKE HOUSES ON A STREET WHERE EACH ADDRESS (INDEX) DIRECTLY LEADS TO A HOUSE.
POINTERS IN A LINKED LIST ARE LIKE FRIENDS PLAYING TELEPHONE - EACH PERSON (NODE) KNOWS ONLY WHO TO TELL NEXT, NOT THE WHOLE CHAIN.
ADDING A NEW PERSON AT THE FRONT OF A LINE - THEY BECOME THE NEW LEADER AND POINT TO WHO WAS PREVIOUSLY FIRST.
REMOVING A MIDDLE BOOK FROM A SHELF BY SLIDING NEIGHBORING BOOKS TOGETHER - BUT IN LINKED LISTS, WE JUST REPOINT THE ARROWS AROUND THE REMOVED BOOK.
A FERRIS WHEEL WHERE EACH CAR (NODE) IS CONNECTED TO THE NEXT. RIDING CONTINUOUSLY BRINGS YOU BACK TO WHERE YOU STARTED.
